name: Spanish C1 Vocabulary
description: Advanced Spanish — CEFR C1 level vocabulary for near-fluent learners

facts.correctAnswer: to be accustomed to doing something
facts.explanation: soler — to be accustomed to doing something.
peor — comparative degree of mal: worse.

facts.correctAnswer: to listen to, to hear out
facts.explanation: escuchar — to listen to, to hear out.
estallar — to explode, to blow up.
lo — the (neuter definite article used before nominalized adjectives).
constar — to be or seem evident.

facts.correctAnswer: A cheer or expression of support
facts.explanation: venga — A cheer or expression of support.

facts.correctAnswer: to have influence or affect on
facts.explanation: incidir — to have influence or affect on.
denegar — to refuse; to turn down.

facts.correctAnswer: to quote or set a price
facts.explanation: cotizar — to quote or set a price.

facts.correctAnswer: to reestablish or promote again the value of something
facts.explanation: reivindicar — to reestablish or promote again the value of something.

facts.correctAnswer: to grab or take hold of
facts.explanation: agarrar — to grab or take hold of.

facts.correctAnswer: a Moorish fortress within or beside a walled city
facts.explanation: alcazaba — a Moorish fortress within or beside a walled city.

facts.correctAnswer: pertaining to the lap or skirt
facts.explanation: faldero — pertaining to the lap or skirt.
redondear — to round; to make round.
cirio — a long, thick wax candle.
consumar — to carry out, to commit.
cotidianidad — day to day, daily life.
esfumar — to soften; to tone down.
fundamentar — to build the basis for.
impactar — to impact, to crash into.

facts.correctAnswer: to build muscle mass by exercising
facts.explanation: muscular — to build muscle mass by exercising.
pues — so, then; in that case.
acomplejar — to give a psychological complex.
adueñar — only used in se ... adueñar.
ahuyentar — to chase away, drive away.

facts.correctAnswer: to cite as a defence or justification for one's actions
facts.explanation: alegar — to cite as a defence or justification for one's actions.
anhelar — to long for, to yearn.
apellidar — to call by one's surname.
arrinconar — to corner, to pin down.

facts.correctAnswer: handle of a vessel or container
facts.explanation: asa — handle of a vessel or container.
averiguar — to find out, figure out.
caracola — shell of a marine snail.

facts.correctAnswer: a kind of large saucepan or saucepot
facts.explanation: cazo — a kind of large saucepan or saucepot.
desistir — to desist, to give up.
desmesurar — to shake up, mess up.

facts.correctAnswer: a small humanoid creature who invades homes at night to
facts.explanation: duende — a small humanoid creature who invades homes at night to.
dueña — female equivalent of dueño: owner.

questionTemplates.questionFormat: Which word is closest in meaning to '{targetLanguageWord}'?
